tagline: Ten full years. Four new teams. One crown.

games.notes:
Darwin's Doom (DD) -- damn! The OG green team came out swinging, with Call of the Wild + Worldly Tutor leading to a fifth-turn Verdant Force. The mega monster swung the balance of the game as saproling tokens poured onto the battlefield and Emerald Army had little to stop him. 2x Yavimaya Ants dealt a handful of damage as well. Great first win of the season for Darwin!

The Emerald Army (TEA) -- honestly I loved their opening hand w/ Pendelhaven, Heart of Yavimaya, Forest, Llanowar Elves, Ice Storm + Wyluli Wolf. They sat on 2x Overrun and Kaysa that would really have broken things open for them quickly had they drawn another land. Encouraging despite the defeat.

Things -- smoked 'em! A turn-3 Buried Alive won them the game, stocking the graveyard with Spirit of the Night and 2x Volcanic Dragon, they drew the other half of the combo the following turn with Living Death resurrecting all three baddies and flipping a Rukh Egg into a Rukh in the process. The black and red build looked nice out there! Game 1 victory in the books!

Holy War -- went defensive-first to buy them some time w/ Meekstone and Kismet hitting the field early, but not early enough. In related news, the Meekstone/Kismet combination is incredibly annoying and may need to be removed from contention. Control themes are just brutal.

Bone Dry. Look at these guys! Win their first ever League contest in, dare I say it, thrilling fashion! There’s a great nostalgic vibe to this crew, watching revised Bad Moon, Will-O-The-Wisp, Demonic Tutor, Mind Twist, a Hyppy and a Dark Ritual do their thing, damn! The Dry heave 2x The Rack + hand discard to crushDnS, and in part thanks to the Furnace of Rath in play. What a great start to their inaugural season. 

DnS. Damn, may have misplayed the Furnace of Rath and likely deployed the DD too fast. Tough call, as it felt like Bone Dry would wipe out their hand in any instant.

Clockwork. Annnnnnd they're back. Clinks and clanks, they deployed 9 artifacts to secure the win. Tetravus played the lead role attacking unimpeded from the air. Triskelion neutralized a Goblin King along the way - that was key. They sat back, withstood an early assault, and countered strong. 

Mean Machine. Tough loss, dispatched a Goblin Recruiter early and misplayed it (i think?). They could have grabbed a few Goblin King for safety, maybe another Balloon Brigade also just to restock the barracks with fighters. Instead, they ran out of gas and sputtered. Quick aside - Goblin Charbelcher is very fun and chaotic. There's also too many high CMC creatures in this deck.

teams.name: Trick or Treat Freaks
teams.identity: Defending champs. Mask-Nought combo anchored by draw engine.
teams.oneliner: Back to defend their title and make it three finals appearances in a row.

teams.name: No Lands For You
teams.identity: Land destruction and hand disruption. Unfinished business after losing the S8 finals.
teams.oneliner: Down to a single Black Vise under restriction, so Nether Void and other legendary foils pick up the slack.

teams.name: Darwin's Doom
teams.identity: Big baddies + draw engine. Going whole-hog into Force of Nature territory.
teams.oneliner: Wild and swingy, promises to be chaotic.

teams.name: Swamp Things
teams.identity: Graveyard resurrection. Ditch blue, load up on hasty haymakers.
teams.oneliner: Downright terrifying with new red additions.

teams.name: Hand Grenade
teams.identity: Underworld Dreams + Yawgmoth Demon + lock artifacts. Maximum per-turn pain.
teams.oneliner: Ol' Yawggy joins a band of misfit artifacts to complement U-Dreams.

teams.name: Dark N Stormy
teams.identity: Mono-red direct damage. Set your hair on fire energy.
teams.oneliner: Same old squad, same old energy. A perfect wrinkle in the league.

teams.name: Mean Machine
teams.identity: All-goblin chaos. Unrecognizable after a trip to the build-a-goblin factory.
teams.oneliner: Wild, reckless, overpowering — now with custom cards from Sir Thomas Steven Kern.

teams.name: Sliver Lining
teams.identity: Season 7 champs. Truly horrifying new free agents added.
teams.oneliner: Added some truly horrifying free agents, compliments of Sir Colten Graham Kern.

teams.name: Folk Music
teams.identity: Merfolk tribal. Mike's annoying AF merman outfit is back again.
teams.oneliner: Rhythmic, deafening and mesmerizing. The Music plays on.

teams.name: Clockwork
teams.identity: All-artifact. The Facts Machine rebrand, still working out the kinks.
teams.oneliner: Nuts and bolts parade. Urzatron-powered mechanical mayhem.

teams.name: Bone Dry
teams.identity: All-out siege on your hand. Bad Ass Fault's best black cards distilled.
teams.oneliner: New team. Pure hand destruction. Your cards are not safe.

teams.name: Demolition Derby
teams.identity: Sacrifice engine built on Lhurgoyf and Grave Pact.
teams.oneliner: The old Sac-and-Recur classic, back in black and green.

teams.name: Holy War
teams.identity: Knights, Thunder Spirits, and more knights. Mono-white aggro.
teams.oneliner: Self-righteous, aggressive, unapologetic — the same holy beatdown returns for another crusade.

teams.name: The Emerald Army
teams.identity: Old-school green elf tribal swarm, now recruiting wolves.
teams.oneliner: The original elf army, now with wolves, wasps, and a grudge.

teams.name: Blue Angels
teams.identity: Serendib Djinn aggro, Moat as the fallback.
teams.oneliner: Four Serendib Djinn hit hard and fast — Land Tax and Lodestone Bauble keep the lights on, Moat and a reset button are there if the race stalls.

teams.name: Womperzoo
teams.identity: Roster and identity to be finalized before Season 9 kickoff.
